weather.tip: Overcast evening at 24.8°C with a 79% chance of rain — carry an umbrella for the commute home and build in extra time if a Metro line or bus route is running behind.

around.headline: ED uncovers $35 million crypto OTC scam in Bengaluru, seizes digital assets worth 8,700 USDT
around.summary: The Enforcement Directorate has uncovered a $35 million over-the-counter crypto scam operating out of Bengaluru and seized digital assets worth 8,700 USDT. Treat informal, in-person or off-exchange crypto deals as high-risk — stick to registered, regulated exchanges for any crypto transaction.

around.headline: Greater Bengaluru Authority officials assaulted during anti-encroachment drive; 10 arrested
around.summary: GBA officials were assaulted while carrying out a footpath-clearance and anti-encroachment drive, and 10 people have been arrested. Expect these enforcement drives to continue in various neighbourhoods, with possible short-notice disruption to footpaths and roadside parking nearby.
around.source: The Times of India / NDTV / The Hindu

around.headline: Namma Metro Green Line services disrupted for 45 minutes after a power breakdown
around.summary: A technical fault in the power supply disrupted Green Line services for about 45 minutes before normal service resumed. If you rely on the Green Line, check live status before heading to the station and keep a backup bus/auto plan for evenings like this.
around.source: The Hindu / Deccan Herald / Public TV English

around.headline: Bengaluru Metro hikes penalties up to Rs 20,000 for nuisance, loud music and trespassing
around.summary: BMRCL has raised fines to as much as Rs 20,000 for offences like creating a nuisance, playing loud music, or trespassing on Metro premises. Factor this into how you (and anyone travelling with you) behave on trains and platforms going forward.

around.headline: Woman loses gold jewellery worth Rs 9 lakh after a wedding-shopping trip
around.summary: A woman has reported losing gold jewellery worth Rs 9 lakh following a wedding-shopping outing in the city. When carrying high-value jewellery for shopping or fittings, keep it on your person at all times, use a bank locker for transport where possible, and avoid leaving bags unattended even briefly.

around.headline: US strikes Iran for a 10th consecutive night as Tehran hits a tanker in the Strait of Hormuz
around.summary: The US-Iran conflict has escalated further, with Iran striking a tanker in the Strait of Hormuz and Kuwait already rationing electricity after Iran hit its power grid. A prolonged Strait of Hormuz disruption is a major oil chokepoint risk, so keep an eye on fuel prices in the coming weeks, and if you have family working in the Gulf, check current travel advisories.

events.name: B.Old Fest
events.note: A month of sport, music and social events curated for Bengaluru's 50+ community — worth a look if it fits you or a parent.

events.name: Science Fest 2026: Enter The Roboverse
events.when: This week, Bhartiya Mall of Bengaluru
events.note: Robotics and AI experiences aimed at kids and families — a solid pick if you've got young ones curious about tech.

events.name: Art Bengaluru Collective — 11th annual art exhibition
events.note: A citywide contemporary art showcase, now in its 11th edition — good for a low-key gallery outing this week.

events.name: Dog adoption drive
events.note: 40 rescued dogs are up for adoption around the city, with 8 already finding homes — worth checking out if you're considering a pet.

street_smart.title: A buyer says "I've just paid you — check your phone" and shows you a screenshot of a UPI payment confirmation, then asks you to hand over the goods right away. What do you do?
street_smart.based_on: Evergreen rotation topic (UPI fraud & fake payment screenshots) — today's feeds included a large crypto OTC scam and a jewellery-loss report, but neither gave enough detail to responsibly build a trap scenario without guessing at the mechanism, so this topic was rotated in instead.
street_smart.situation: You're selling something in person — at a shop counter, on a resale app, or informally — and the buyer shows you what looks like a successful UPI payment screenshot, then presses you to release the item quickly because they're 'in a hurry'.

choices.text: Politely ask them to wait, open your own bank or UPI app (or wait for the SMS/bank alert), confirm the exact amount has actually credited, and only then hand over the goods.
choices.feedback: Correct. A screenshot is just an image — it can be faked, edited, or show a payment that's still 'processing' rather than completed. The only proof that matters is the credit showing up in your own account.

choices.text: Accept the screenshot as proof and hand over the goods immediately since the payment 'sound' played and the amount looked right.
choices.feedback: This is exactly how fake-payment scams work: fraudulent apps and edited screenshots can perfectly mimic a real payment confirmation, sound included. By the time you check your actual balance and find nothing credited, the buyer and the goods are both gone.

choices.text: Glance at a notification banner on your phone that pops up, see a rupee amount, and take that as confirmation without opening the banking app itself.
choices.feedback: Notification banners can be spoofed by lookalike apps or misread at a glance. Always open your actual banking or UPI app and check the transaction history, not just a banner notification.

choices.text: Ask the buyer to send a second screenshot as extra proof before handing over the item.
choices.feedback: A second faked screenshot is no more trustworthy than the first — it comes from the same source. Verification has to come from your own bank or UPI app, not from anything the other party shows you.

takeaways: Never release goods or cash based on a screenshot alone — always verify the credit directly in your own bank or UPI app, or via an official SMS/bank alert.
Fake payment apps and edited screenshots can convincingly copy real ones, including the confirmation sound, so treat urgency from the other party as a warning sign, not a reason to skip verification.
For high-value or first-time transactions, prefer waiting a minute for your bank's own alert over trusting anything shown on someone else's phone.

quiz.q: How many people were arrested for assaulting Greater Bengaluru Authority officials during an anti-encroachment drive today?
quiz.why: The officials were attacked while carrying out a footpath-clearance drive, and such enforcement drives are expected to continue across the city.

quiz.q: For roughly how long were Namma Metro Green Line services disrupted today after a power breakdown?
quiz.why: Services resumed after the technical fault in the power supply was fixed — worth checking live status before you head to the station on a day like this.

quiz.q: India summoned a senior diplomat from which country today over the deaths of four Indian nationals on a ship?
quiz.why: The four Indians were killed in an attack on a ship at Ukraine's Odesa port in the Black Sea; the summons followed as a diplomatic response.

quiz.q: Which Article of the Indian Constitution empowers the President to declare a National Emergency?
quiz.why: Article 356, by contrast, deals with President's Rule when a state government fails constitutionally — a commonly confused pair.

quiz.q: Roughly what percentage of Earth's atmosphere, by volume, is nitrogen?
quiz.why: Oxygen, the gas we most associate with breathing, makes up only about 21% — most of the air around us during Bengaluru's rains is actually nitrogen.
